metadata.description: Stride task management skills for AI agents

plugins.name: stride
plugins.description: Task lifecycle skills for Stride kanban: claiming, completing, creating tasks and goals, enriching tasks, workflow orchestration, subagent dispatch, and automatic hook execution via Claude Code hooks. v1.15.0+: `capture_changed_files()` reflects the agent's full working state at completion time, regardless of commit state — committed, staged, modified-uncommitted, AND untracked-new files all surface in the snapshot, with untracked text files appearing as synthesized new-file unified patches; the canonical API Request Format example in skills/stride-completing-tasks/SKILL.md now inlines the snapshot read inside the curl invocation (via `jq -n --argjson cf "$(cat "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.stride-changed-files.json" 2>/dev/null || echo '[]')"`) so the read happens AFTER the PreToolUse-on-complete hook has written the file; the kanban-repo `docs/diff-contract.md` encoding guidance was updated in lockstep to document the working-tree-relative semantic. v1.14.0+: implements the G148/W719 per-file diff JSON contract — the after_doing hook captures changed files per the contract, truncates at exactly 500 lines with the contract marker, emits the binary placeholder for files git marks binary in --numstat, and writes the resulting JSON array to .stride-changed-files.json for the completion payload to carry as the optional `changed_files` field. v1.14.1+: the canonical API Request Format example body and the pre-completion verification checklist in skills/stride-completing-tasks/SKILL.md both surface `changed_files` directly, so agents reading the standard example are prompted to embed the snapshot during normal payload assembly. Reviewers see per-file unified-patch text inline in the Stride review queue. Backward-compatible — legacy completions without changed_files continue to validate. v1.13.0+: the task-reviewer agent emits a structured, parseable JSON review-report block alongside the prose summary, so downstream tooling can extract per-severity issue counts, per-issue file:line locations, and acceptance-criteria coverage.

plugins.name: stride-security-review
plugins.description: AI-powered security review via /stride-security-review:security-review. Two scan modes: diff (default, PR-gating) and --full (codebase posture check, batched with cross-batch dedup and skipped-files reporting). Detects 10 universal vulnerability classes plus 5 MAESTRO-derived agentic-AI classes (prompt injection, tool abuse, agent trust boundary, model output execution, vector-store poisoning). Seven framework rule packs (new in 2.3.0): Android/Kotlin (exported components, WebView JS bridge RCE, cleartext traffic, SharedPreferences credentials, SQLite injection, trust-all SSL), Django/Python (mark_safe, raw/extra SQL, CSRF, production settings, mass-assignment, open-redirect, deserialization, SSRF, DRF mass-assignment), Express/Node.js (reflected XSS, eval RCE, child_process injection, Mongoose NoSQL injection, prototype pollution), iOS/Swift (UserDefaults credentials, WKWebView file access, ATS arbitrary loads, URLSession trust-all, deep-link missing auth), Phoenix/Elixir (raw/1, force_ssl, CSRF, fragment interpolation, LiveView scope, mass-assignment, Token replay, System.cmd shell, LiveView upload guards, open-redirect), Rails/Ruby (html_safe, find_by_sql, CSRF, params.permit!, eval/send, open-redirect, deserialization, connection.execute, missing-auth, JSON-leak), React/Next.js (dangerouslySetInnerHTML, API route missing auth, redirect open-redirect, javascript: href scheme, getServerSideProps secret leak). Plus a multi-platform CI/CD pipeline pack (8 platforms) and a framework-agnostic Web defense-in-depth pack covering security headers (CSP/HSTS/X-Frame-Options/cookie flags). Every finding carries CWE-ID and OWASP Top 10 references. Opt-in flags: --maestro (7-layer threat classification with explicit non-AI mapping), --rci [N] (recursive criticism passes), --baseline (suppress acknowledged findings), --patches (surgical-fix unified diffs), --fail-on <severity> (CI exit-code gate), --base <ref> (PR-against-base diff scope), --sarif (SARIF v2.1.0 output for GitHub Code Scanning). Ships a reference GitHub Actions workflow and a TAP 13 eval runner over 64 fixtures with positive-and-negative-control coverage. Semantic analysis, not pattern matching. Loosely based on anthropics/claude-code-security-review.

plugins.name: stride-ideation
plugins.description: Turn a fuzzy idea into shipped Stride tasks via two slash commands. /stride-ideation:ideate drives an interactive round-based question loop (up to 4 batched AskUserQuestion per round, mandatory round-3 framing checkpoint, mandatory round-4 premortem in v0.3.0, seven hard-gated requirements sections with shape requirements on Assumptions — ranked highest-to-lowest risk with the riskiest marked — and Success Metrics — both leading and lagging indicators required — advisory requirements-reviewer pass) and writes a committed requirements markdown doc — the terminal state, never auto-funnels into the next step. New in v0.4.0: a --profile <lean|product|discovery> flag selects the round structure and reviewer rubric (lean = default, byte-for-byte v0.3.0; product = JTBD four-forces in Round 1 + optional Concrete Example section; discovery = Why-now + Alternative-options in Round 2). New in v0.5.0: a fourth --profile=lean-startup value adds a mandatory Round-5 MVP-design batch anchored on the (R)-marked Assumptions entry, an optional MVP / Validation experiment section in the doc, and two profile-aware advisory reviewer checks (MVP section presence, falsifiable success/failure criteria) — source techniques: Eric Ries (Lean Startup), Steve Blank (Customer Development), Giff Constable (Riskiest Assumption Test). /stride-ideation:stridify (new in v0.2.0; merged from the previous /decompose + /ship pair) takes the requirements doc and runs the full pipeline end-to-end: validates the seven sections, preflights auth from .stride_auth.md, dispatches the requirements-decomposer subagent (canonical batch shape embedded; two-reason multi-goal split rule of code-coupling plus ~10-task soft cap; cross-goal claim ordering documented in decomposition_notes), stamps source_spec + source_spec_sha256, writes and commits a sibling timestamped batch JSON for audit, then strips local-audit fields and POSTs to /api/tasks/batch with token hygiene (no curl -v, no token in logs or errors), rendering the created G/W identifiers. Replaces superpowers:brainstorming for Stride projects; the two skills coexist for non-Stride projects where brainstorming remains the right fit. New in v0.7.0: a four-layer resilience model on /stridify — (1) a bounded subagent-dispatch retry in Step 7 (3 attempts, ~30s/~90s backoff on HTTP 529 / transient network / 'overloaded' classifications; terminal classifications still fail fast), (2) a new --goal <name|index> flag that scopes the decomposer prompt to one surface from the doc's ## Decomposition seams section (accepts integer indices AND slug-matching strings; both --goal value and --goal=value parse forms; the seams section remains freeform and ungated), (3) a retry-exhaustion fallback (new Step 7.5) that writes the assembled prompt + verbatim last error + recovery README to a sibling *-decomposer-prompt.md file when 3 transient failures pile up (Stride API POST is NOT attempted in this branch), and (4) a one-line preflight advisory in Step 2 when a doc enumerates more than 3 surfaces under ## Decomposition seams and --goal is not set. The Stride API POST itself is still NOT retried — partial-batch idempotency is not guaranteed; the user remains the retry mechanism on a 4xx/5xx. Test coverage grows from 59 to 109 assertions (3 new suites: test-stridify-retry, test-stridify-per-goal, test-stridify-fallback). Back-compat is preserved on the happy path — v0.6.0 users see byte-identical behavior unless they invoke --goal or hit a transient subagent failure.
